folds.keeper: Mullá Muḥammad, son of Mullá Taqí
merged: Mullá Muḥammad, son of Mullá Taqí, S̱hahíd-i-Ṯháliṯh
folds.reason: Both are Ṭáhirih's cousin/husband, son of Mullá Taqí; the S̱hahíd-i-Ṯháliṯh entry is the same man (the title the shí‘ahs gave Mullá Taqí's son). Seed 'Mullá Muḥammad' (mujtahid of Núr) and the Míyámay martyrs are distinct and kept separate.

folds.keeper: Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí (Letter of the Living, martyr of S̱hayḵh Ṭabarsí)
merged: Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí (Letter of the Living, brother-in-law of Ṭáhirih)
folds.reason: All three are the one Letter of the Living: son of Ḥájí Mullá ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb of Qazvín, husband of Ṭáhirih's sister Marḍíyyih, martyred at Ṭabarsí. Seed 'Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí' (the Covenant-breaker half-brother of ‘Abdu’l-Bahá) and 'son of Siyyid Aḥmad' (young fort martyr) are different men, kept separate.
All three are the Iṣfahán Mírzá Hádí, brother of the Nahrí (Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alíy-i-Nahrí = the Siyyid-i-Nahrí). Kept separate: 'son of Mullá ‘Abdu’l-Vahháb-i-Qazvíní' (different father, Qazvín).

folds.keeper: Ḥasan (bearer of the body of Mullá Ḥusayn)
folds.reason: Same young Ḵhurásání at Ṭabarsí who, with Qulí, rescued the wounded and bore Mullá Ḥusayn's body to the fort. Kept separate: 'Ḥasan (servant of Vaḥíd)' (Nayríz/Yazd context, different man).
Both denote the Báb (seed, 783 mentions). Kept separate: 'Siyyid ‘Alí-Muḥammad (son of Vaḥíd)' (a child of Vaḥíd, distinct person).

merged: Ḥájí Muḥammad-Taqí (of Nayríz, mentioned in the Súriy-i-Ayyúb)
folds.reason: Seed is surnamed Ayyúb — i.e. the long-suffering one of Nayríz mentioned in the Súriy-i-Ayyúb; the two DB entries (Nayríz, mentioned in Súriy-i-Ayyúb / Nayríz martyr renowned for justice) are the same man.
Both are the brother of Siyyid Káẓim-i-Zanjání, one of the Seven Martyrs of Ṭihrán. Kept separate: 'Siyyid Murtaḍá (martyr of Zanján)' (merchant of Zanján who died in the Zanján upheaval).

folds.keeper: Qulí (bearer of the body of Mullá Ḥusayn)
merged: Qulí (companion of Ḵhurásán, wounded at Ṭabarsí)
folds.reason: Same young Ḵhurásání Qulí who rode in advance of Quddús, was wounded, and bore Mullá Ḥusayn's body to the fort. Single individual across all three entries.

Both are the younger brother of Mullá Ḥusayn (given the Bábu'l-Báb's sword/turban by Quddús). Kept separate: 'philosopher of the school of Mullá Ṣadrá' (an inquirer who questioned the Báb about miracles, different man).
Transliteration variant (Bus̱hrú’í = Bushrú’í) of the same person — Mullá Ḥusayn, the Bábu'l-Báb, first Letter of the Living. Seed keeper (216 mentions).
Both are the merchant of Káshán who hosted the Báb for three nights. Same person.
All three are the distinguished Qazvín mujtahid, father of the two sons (one being the Letter of the Living Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí), addressed in a Tablet by the Báb. Same person.
Both are the Tabríz custodian to whom the Báb's Tablets/commentaries were entrusted for concealment. Same person.

renames.from: Mírzá Ḥasan (imám of a quarter of Yazd)
